title: eriogonum heracleoides var. utahensis
intro: Eriogonum heracleoides var. utahensis, commonly known as Utah Buckwheat, is a perennial shrub that belongs to the Polygonaceae family and is native to the southwestern United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Utah Buckwheat include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and support immune function. The herbal preparations of Eriogonum heracleoides var. utahensis can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can be used externally as a poultice or salve to aid in wound healing and skin conditions.
